candidate_problems.text: Can the 'low-entropy past as public good' analogy be made into a precise explanatory framework rather than a metaphor? In particular, what are the exact stocks, flows, access conditions, and depletion dynamics of this informational resource across subsystems, and does this framing generate novel, testable distinctions from standard Past Hypothesis accounts of thermodynamic asymmetry?

What explains the origin of the special low-entropy boundary condition itself, without merely redescribing it as brute fact? This remains the central unresolved problem: whether cosmology, quantum gravity, inflationary scenarios, selection effects, or some deeper lawlike/meta-law structure can non-circularly account for why the universe began in such an extraordinarily atypical macrostate.

Why do multiple arrows—thermodynamic, psychological, causal, computational, evolutionary, and cosmological—align so robustly, and are there principled conditions under which they could decouple? The conjecture says they are coordinated because they exploit the same global resource, but it leaves open whether this alignment is necessary, contingent, or only approximate, and what empirical or theoretical cases would reveal divergence.
